primary_reason: The study combines difference-in-differences and regression discontinuity designs to estimate the population impact and comparative effectiveness of a two-dose varicella vaccination policy.
The review identifies diagnostic and cross-sector surveillance gaps for zoonotic tuberculosis and prescribes an integrated One Health surveillance and risk-mitigation framework.
The qualitative study concerns asbestos-related occupational disease and supplies no concrete route for transferring its approach to infectious-disease epidemiology.
The review and proposed checklist address a rare genetic syndrome without a concrete infectious-disease application or transfer route.
The review evaluates sequencing-based diagnostic performance, sampling choices, implementation barriers, and surveillance applications for Orthoflavivirus infections.
The cross-sectional study combines serological and epidemiological observations to estimate chikungunya attack rate beyond reported case counts.
The study quantifies antimicrobial-susceptibility misclassification caused by outdated breakpoints and demonstrates how updated CLSI thresholds correct resistance surveillance results.
The report evaluates strengthened routine pharmacovigilance across three malaria-vaccine programs and derives transferable lessons about adverse-event reporting and investigation.
The study uses a discrete-time Markov simulation with underreporting adjustment to estimate population vaccination impacts.
The study calibrates a mechanistic SARS-CoV-2 transmission and immunity model to serology to estimate changing underreporting and intervention effects.
The narrative review summarizes mpox epidemiology, treatments, preparedness, and prevention without identifying a reusable analytic or measurement contribution.
The disproportionality analysis concerns opioid abuse and dependence and does not state a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The analysis primarily evaluates the general adverse-event profile of a lupus medicine and does not provide a concrete infectious-disease transfer route.
The study descriptively reports needlestick frequency, reporting, and follow-up without a distinct transferable design or measurement contribution.
The review describes geographic patterns of autoimmune and immunodeficiency disorders without a concrete infectious-disease methodological transfer route.
The study uses multiple imputation to reconstruct missing tuberculosis risk-group information and adjust estimates of latent-infection prevalence in vulnerable populations.
The study integrates sequencing, phylogenetic analysis, and population serology to characterize variant introductions and circulation.
The scoping review systematically maps gonorrhoea prevalence, resistance evidence, and laboratory-testing capacity to inform treatment rollout and sentinel surveillance design.
The review combines random-effects prevalence meta-analysis with a structured One Health narrative synthesis of anthrax outbreak surveillance.
The case demonstrates cerebrospinal-fluid metagenomic sequencing with bioinformatic contamination exclusion as an etiologic diagnostic strategy for an underrecognized infection.
The paper develops and stress-tests a forecasting pipeline that fuses empirical visibility graphs with stochastic simulations and compares it against standard forecasting models.
The pilot evaluates paper-based syndromic surveillance and wastewater-monitoring feasibility as complementary approaches for detecting infections in daycare centers.
The guideline standardizes prehospital sepsis recognition thresholds, treatment pathways, clinical records, and alert activation procedures.
The study evaluates wastewater RT-qPCR, sequencing, and shedding-sensitive burden models as complementary surveillance tools for dengue and chikungunya.
Mechanistic models integrate genetic and epidemiological data to estimate severity and transmission.
The review broadly summarizes Salmonella epidemiology, pathogenesis, resistance, vaccines, and surveillance without a substantive reusable methodological contribution.
The hospital cohort uses standard regression and survival analyses to describe respiratory-virus testing and outcomes without a distinct transferable design contribution.
The scoping review systematically compares surveillance-based, computational, machine-learning, and Bayesian geospatial methods for estimating acute infectious-disease burden.
The study audits antimicrobial-trial reporting completeness and timeliness to evaluate regulatory effects on research transparency.
The study develops and evaluates an LLM pipeline for extracting pertussis signals from free text and tests its incremental forecasting value across multiple models.
The review compares chikungunya detection limitations and mitigation options and proposes deployable diagnostic and control strategies for resource-limited settings.
The study tests digital-PCR wastewater detection with fecal normalization as a complementary population surveillance strategy for Borrelia burgdorferi.
The analysis uses standard joinpoint methods to describe mortality trends and disparities without adding a reusable infectious-disease design or measurement strategy.
The study triangulates prospective screening, phylogenetic reconstruction, meta-analysis, and transmission modeling to estimate cryptic mpox infections and their transmission contribution.
The study applies a combined Markov and decision-tree model with scenario and sensitivity analyses to project COVID-19 vaccination effects and cost-effectiveness.
The cross-sectional clinic study estimates HTLV-1 prevalence with standard serological confirmation but offers no distinct reusable design or measurement contribution.
The paper proves structural unidentifiability in an underreported SIR model and uses stochastic simulation to show how survey data restore parameter identifiability.
The cross-sectional knowledge, attitudes, and practices survey describes behavioral and service barriers to pediatric tuberculosis diagnosis without a distinct transferable method contribution.
The review primarily concerns smoking, alcohol, and diabetes management and supplies no concrete infectious-epidemiology transfer route.
The study combines longitudinal symptom measurement, multiple imputation, longitudinal k-means, and hierarchical clustering to derive reproducible long-COVID burden trajectories and phenotypes.
The case concerns a suspected drug-induced blood disorder, and the coincident SARS-CoV-2 infection does not create a concrete infectious-disease methodological transfer route.
The study uses a deterministic compartmental model and counterfactual intervention scenarios to distinguish changes in HIV transmission, population size, and testing behavior.
This is a study protocol with planned analyses but no completed methodological evaluation, diagnostic result, correction, guidance, or reproducibility finding.
The study uses quasi-Poisson time-series attribution and sensitivity analyses to infer RSV and influenza hospital burden from nonspecific respiratory-admission data.
The scoping review diagnoses heterogeneous and incomplete consent reporting in severe-infection trials and calls for standardized reporting of recruitment loss.
The systematic search uses the geographic distribution of ceftriaxone-resistance isolates to diagnose major blind spots and differential capacity in gonococcal AMR surveillance.
The narrative overview summarizes chikungunya burden and control challenges in India without a substantive reusable analytic, measurement, or evidence-synthesis method.
The study audits adverse-event report completeness and compares two causality-assessment systems to show how reporting quality affects pharmacovigilance conclusions.
The review develops criteria for a neuropsychiatric syndrome and does not establish a concrete transfer route to infectious-disease epidemiology.
The cohort supports a structured biovigilance framework using microbiological investigation and donor-sample traceability to assess infectious events after fecal microbiota transplantation.
The study combines Global Burden of Disease estimates, standardized trend measures, and ARIMA forecasting to characterize and project HPV-attributable cervical-cancer burden.

The comparative ecological study integrates segmented malaria trends with governance, economic, surveillance, and concurrent-disease indicators to assess health-system resilience.

Integrated occupancy and demographic compartmental models estimate spillover, infections, and surveillance gaps.

The culture-confirmed case series describes clinical presentations of neuromelioidosis without a distinct transferable diagnostic or epidemiological method.
